quotes.translation: 在那特定午后之前与之后，同一时刻的不同午后，还有无数其他事件发生在你身上。你手掌中的细胞包含着令你意识心智目眩神迷的记忆。但请记住，你二十七岁手掌中的细胞，在物理上绝非体验过那些事件的同一批细胞。然而在某种感官深处，那些无数"过去"午后所经历的刺激与反应的埋藏证据依然存在。其中某些记忆必将回放，影响你视为二十七岁当前体验的事物。你的意识思想与习惯调节着哪些记忆会融入当下的漩涡。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: When you are determined to manipulate your environment, then you separate yourself from it. Since you are part of it, this also leads you to try to place yourself apart from your own subjective reality. It is quite possible to take your normally conscious "I" into the dream state, to your advantage. When you do this you will see that the dreaming "I" and the waking "I" are one, but operating in entirely different environments. Therefore, you become familiar with depths of experience and knowledge unknown to you before. You acquire a true flexibility and expanded awareness of your own being, and open channels of communication between your waking and dreaming realities. This means that you are far better able to utilize unconscious knowledge, and also to acquaint the unconscious with your present physical situation.
quotes.translation: 当你决心操控环境时，便将自己与环境分离。由于你本是环境的一部分，这也会导致你试图将自己与自身主观实相分离。将你通常有意识的"我"带入梦境状态是完全可能的，且对你有利。当你如此做时，会发现做梦的"我"与醒时的"我"本为一体，只是运作于全然不同的环境。因此，你将熟悉此前未知的体验与知识深度。你获得真正的灵活性，扩展对自身存在的觉知，并开启清醒与梦境实相之间的沟通渠道。这意味着你能更有效地运用无意识知识，同时让无意识了解你当前的物质处境。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: (11:02.) Give us a moment ... I told you (in the last session) that in one probability Ruburt was a nun, expressing mysticism in a highly disciplined context, where it must be watched so that it does not get out of hand. Because there is an unconscious flow of information and experience here, you have one of the reasons for Ruburt's caution in some psychic matters, and his fear of leading people astray. There were three offshoots: one, the nun, with mysticism conventionally expressed, but under guarded circumstances; one, the writer who veiled mystical experience through art; and one, the Ruburt you know, who experienced mystical experience directly, teaches others to do the same, and forms through writing a wedding of the two aspects. You have known two of those selves, then, and you were present at Ruburt's birth with Idea Construction.
quotes.translation: （11:02分）稍等片刻……我（在上次课程中）告诉过你，在某个概率中，鲁柏曾是一位修女，在高度受约束的环境中表达神秘主义，这种表达必须受到严密监控以防失控。由于此处存在无意识的信息与经验流动，这便成为鲁柏在某些通灵事务中保持谨慎、并担心误导他人的原因之一。存在三个分支：其一，以传统方式表达神秘主义却受严密监控的修女；其二，通过艺术掩盖神秘体验的作家；其三，你所认识的鲁柏——他直接体验神秘体验，教导他人同样实践，并通过写作将这两个面向结合。那么，你已认识其中两个自我，并在鲁柏诞生时以"意念建构"的身份在场。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 680
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: The concepts in such a system as this can help break those barriers. There are, then, stratas of consciousness existing at once. The ones you are not aware of yet seem more progressed, developed than your own. You are a part of them now. You can know them as you begin to stretch your concepts of personhood and awareness. In terms of time you have many bodies, as you are born and reborn in earth experience. Your consciousness straddles those existences, and even the atoms and molecules within your present body contain the coded knowledge of those other (really simultaneous) forms. These units of consciousness are within all physical matter, containing their own memories. Both biologically and psychically, then, you are aware of your multipersonhood.
quotes.translation: 此类体系中的概念能帮助打破那些屏障。因此，存在着同时存在的意识层。那些你尚未觉察的层次，似乎比你的意识更进步、更发达。你现在就是它们的一部分。当你开始扩展对人格与觉知的概念时，便能认识它们。就时间而言，你拥有许多身体，因为你在地球经验中不断出生与重生。你的意识跨越那些存在，甚至你当前身体内的原子与分子，都包含着那些其他（实为同时的）形态的编码知识。这些意识单位存在于所有物质中，携带着自身的记忆。因此，无论从生物学还是灵性层面，你都意识到自己的多重人格。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 683
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: Give us a moment ... There are inner structures within matter. These are swirls of energy. They have more purposes than one. The structures are formed by organizations of consciousness, or CU's. You have the most intimate knowledge of the nature of a cell, for example, or of an atom. They compose your flesh. There is, in certain terms, a continuum of consciousness there of which your present physical life is a part. You are in certain kinds of communication and communion with your own cells, and at certain levels of consciousness you know this. A true physicist would learn to reach that level of consciousness at will. There were pictures drawn of cellular structures long before any technological methods of seeing them were available, in your terms.
quotes.translation: 稍等片刻……物质内部存在内在结构。这些是能量的漩涡。它们具有多重目的。这些结构由意识组织或CU（意识单位）构成。例如，你对细胞或原子的本质有着最亲密的认知。它们构成了你的血肉。在特定意义上，那里存在着意识的连续体，你当前的物质生命是其一部分。你在某种交流与共融中与自身细胞互动，并在某些意识层面知晓这一点。真正的物理学家会学会随意达到那个意识层面。在你看来，早在任何技术手段能观察到细胞结构之前，就已有人画出它们的图像。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 701
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: "Several interesting -- and frustrating -- questions are raised by today's episode. As stated, this makes the second time that I've had an experience involving the violent death of a Roman soldier in the earlier part of the first century A.D. (I never did arrive at names for those two militant individuals.) Perhaps both instances are merely my own psychological reflections of present concerns or challenges, although I think that more is involved. Given Seth's concept of simultaneous time, the best connection I've made so far between the two soldiers is that as counterparts of mine they explore questions having to do with authority. As I rebel against authority now -- a characteristic remarked upon by Seth in the 721st session -- so do my Roman selves in their times.
quotes.translation: "今日的片段引发了几个有趣且令人困惑的问题。如前所述，这是我第二次经历涉及公元一世纪早期一名罗马士兵惨死的事件（我始终未能为那两位好斗的个体命名）。或许这两个实例都只是我对当前关切或挑战的心理投射，尽管我认为其中涉及更多。鉴于赛斯的同时性时间概念，我目前能在这两名士兵之间建立的最佳联系是：作为我的对应体，他们探索与权威相关的问题。正如我现在反抗权威——赛斯在第721节课程中曾指出这一特质——我的罗马自我们也在他们的时代如此行事。"
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (FOR SESSION 724)
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: There seems to be a division between science and religion, for even organized religion has an intuitive basis. The male scientist is often ashamed of using his intuitions, for not only do they appear to be unscientific, but female as well. It is what others will think about his masculinity that such a man is concerned with. To be "illogical" is a scientific "crime" -- not so much because it is an unscientific attribute, but because it is considered a feminine one. Science has followed the male orientation and become its epitome. Up until the present, science has consistently tried to do without the so-called feminine qualities. It has divorced knowledge from emotion, understanding from identification, and stressed sexuality over personhood.
quotes.translation: 科学与宗教之间似乎存在分野，因为即便有组织的宗教也拥有直觉基础。男性科学家常羞于运用直觉，这不仅因为直觉显得不科学，更因其被视为女性特质。这类男性所担忧的是他人对其男子气概的看法。成为"不合逻辑"是科学上的"罪行"——与其说这是不科学的属性，不如说它被视为女性化特质。科学遵循男性导向并成为其缩影。直至当前，科学始终试图摒弃所谓的女性特质。它将知识与情感分离，将理解与认同割裂，并强调性别凌驾于人格之上。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Any true psychic development of personality, however, is bound to lead to an understanding of the nature of the psyche that is far too large for any such confusion of basic identity with sexuality. The concept of reincarnation itself clearly shows the change of sexual orientation, and the existence of a self that is apart from its sexual orientation, even while it is also expressed through a given sexual stance. To a good extent, sexual beliefs are responsible for the blocking-out of reincarnational awareness. Such "memory" would necessarily acquaint you with experiences most difficult to correlate with your current sexual roles. Those other-sex existences are present to the psyche unconsciously. They are a portion of your personality. In so specifically identifying with your sex, therefore, you also inhibit memories that might limit or destroy that identification.
quotes.translation: 然而，任何真正的人格通灵发展，都必然导向对心灵本质的理解——这种本质远非将基本身份与性别混淆所能涵盖。轮回概念本身清晰地展示了性取向的变化，以及一个独立于性取向的自我存在——即便它同时通过特定性别立场来表达。在很大程度上，性别信念是阻碍轮回觉知的原因。这种"记忆"必然让你接触到与当前性别角色最难协调的经验。那些异性别存在无意识地存在于心灵中。它们是你人格的一部分。因此，当你如此具体地认同自身性别时，也会抑制那些可能限制或摧毁这种认同的记忆。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: When your eyes are on the road of time, therefore, you forget the circular motion of your being. When you dream or sleep, however, the world of cause and effect either vanishes or appears confused and chaotic. Normal daytime images are mixed and matched, so that combinations are formed quite different from those seen in the daylight. The known rules that govern the behavior of creatures and objects in dreams seem no longer to apply. Past, present, and future merge in a seemingly bizarre alliance in which, were you waking, you would lose all mental footing. The circular nature of the psyche to some extent makes itself known. When you think of dreams you usually consider those aspects of it only, commenting perhaps upon the strange activities, the odd juxtapositions and the strange character of dream life itself. Few are struck by the fact of their dreams' own order, or impressed by the ultimate restraint that allows such sometimes-spectacular events to occur in such a relatively restricted physical framework.
quotes.translation: 因此，当你的目光专注于时间之路时，你便忘记了自己存在的循环运动。然而，当你做梦或睡眠时，因果世界要么消失，要么显得混乱无序。白天的正常意象被混合搭配，形成与日光下所见截然不同的组合。支配梦中生物与物体行为的已知规则似乎不再适用。过去、现在与未来以一种看似怪异的联盟融合在一起，若你清醒，便会失去一切心理立足点。心灵的本质在某种程度上彰显了其循环特性。当你想到梦境时，通常只考虑这些方面，或许会评论其奇怪的活动、奇特的并置以及梦生活本身的奇异特征。很少有人被梦境自身的秩序所打动，或对那种允许如此壮观事件发生在相对受限的物理框架内的终极约束印象深刻。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: But nowhere do we see anything about any spiritual dilemmas that may be involved with all of this, or about the enormous social problems -- challenges, to be sure -- that could soon begin to manifest themselves if anything even approaching "eternal life" comes within the reach of numbers of people. Think of the legalities alone involved. (People might even have to change their marriage vows!) Science-fiction ideas abound. What about population control? What about those who want to live on, but can't afford to? How will decisions be made about who receives the favored treatment and who doesn't? Will families qualify, or just individuals? Geniuses or dolts? If the services necessary to extend life are free -- paid for by the government, that is -- will government decide that certain families simply cannot be allowed to have children, that they'll be left to die out? In view of our present world challenges, it might even be said that there are already too many people in the world. And what about animals and other forms of life? Perhaps in their own collective wisdom the animals will look upon us as though we've altogether given up our powers of intuitive understanding.
quotes.translation: 但我们在任何地方都看不到关于这一切可能涉及的任何精神困境，或关于巨大的社会问题——当然是挑战——如果任何接近“永生”的事物开始为众多人所能触及，这些问题可能很快显现。仅想想涉及的法律问题。（人们甚至可能不得不改变他们的婚姻誓言！）科幻小说的想法比比皆是。人口控制怎么办？那些想要活下去但负担不起的人怎么办？如何决定谁接受优待，谁不接受？是家庭有资格，还是只有个人？天才还是傻瓜？如果延长生命所需的服务是免费的——即由政府支付——政府是否会决定某些家庭根本不允许生孩子，让他们自生自灭？鉴于我们目前的世界挑战，甚至可以说世界上已经有太多人了。动物和其他生命形式呢？也许动物以其集体智慧，会视我们为完全放弃了直觉理解能力的存在。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: NOTES: SESSION 803
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: He was filled with wonder as his own consciousness ever-newly came into being. He had not yet covered over that process with the kind of smooth continuity that your own consciousness has now achieved -- so when he thought a thought he was filled with curiosity: Where had it come from? His own consciousness, then, was forever a source of delight, its changing qualities as noticeable and apparent as the changing sky. The relative smoothness of your own consciousness -- in those terms, at least -- was gained at the expense of certain other experiences, therefore, that were possible otherwise. You could not live in your present world of time if your consciousness was as playful, curious, and creative as it was, for [then] time was also experienced far differently.
quotes.translation: 他充满了惊奇，因为自己的意识不断焕然一新地诞生。他尚未用你们意识现在所拥有的那种平滑连续性覆盖这个过程——所以当他思考一个念头时，他充满了好奇：它从何而来？于是，他的意识永远是喜悦的源泉，其变化的特质如同变化的天空一样明显可见。你们意识的相对平滑——至少从那些角度来看——是以牺牲某些其他体验为代价获得的，否则这些体验本可能实现。如果你们的意识像过去那样顽皮、好奇且富有创造力，你们就无法生活在现在的时空中，因为那时时间的体验也截然不同。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Regardless of your histories, those early men and women were quite healthy. They had strong teeth and bones. They dealt with the physical world through the purposeful use of the imagination, however, in a way now most difficult to understand. They realized they were mortal, and must die, but their greater awareness of Framework 2 allowed them a larger identification, so they understood that death was not only a natural necessity, but also an opportunity for other kinds of experience and development (see Note 1 for Session 803).
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Some people are only aware of -- or largely aware of -- impulses toward anger, because they have inhibited those natural impulses toward love that would otherwise temper what seemed to be aggressive desires. When you begin trusting yourselves, you start by taking it for granted that to some extent at least you have not trusted yourself or your impulses in the past: You have thought that impulses were dangerous, disruptive, or even evil. So as you begin to learn self-trust, you acknowledge your impulses. You try them on for size. You see where they lead you by allowing them some freedom. You do not follow urges through that would hurt others physically, or that seem in direct contradiction to your present beliefs -- but you do acknowledge them. You do try to discover their source. Behind them you will almost always find an inhibited impulse -- or many of them -- that motivated you to move in some ideal direction, to seek a love or understanding so idealized in your mind that it seemed impossible to achieve. You are left with the impulse to strike out.
quotes.translation: 有些人只意识到——或主要意识到——愤怒的冲动，因为他们抑制了那些自然的爱的冲动，否则这些冲动会缓和看似攻击性的欲望。当你开始信任自己时，你首先会理所当然地认为，至少在一定程度上，你过去没有信任自己或自己的冲动：你认为冲动是危险的、破坏性的，甚至是邪恶的。因此，当你开始学习自我信任时，你承认自己的冲动。你试着感受它们的大小。你通过给予它们一些自由，看看它们会把你引向何方。你不会遵循那些会伤害他人身体、或与你当前信念直接矛盾的冲动——但你确实承认它们。你确实试图发现它们的来源。在它们背后，你几乎总会发现一个被抑制的冲动——或许多个——它们激励你朝着某个理想方向前进，去寻求一种在你心中如此理想化以至于似乎无法实现的爱或理解。你最终只剩下出击的冲动。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 872, AUGUST 8, 1979
9:15 P.M. WEDNESDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: It would be impossible at this time, I'm certain, for a researcher to find any evidence that reincarnational heritages are coded for among the approximately 100,000 genes lined up on the 46 chromosomes we carry in the nucleus of each of our cells. We say that a certain gene contains the instructions for the manufacture of a certain protein the body uses in the construction or function of an eye, for instance, and that in expressing that code the gene passes on characteristics inherited from physical ancestors--but is that endowment influenced or directed in any fashion by reincarnational attributes as well? Might those factors be just as potent as those inherited from a grandfather, say? The genes in each cell have their individual jobs to do in furnishing the quivering templates for the manufacture (via the nucleic acids DNA and messenger RNA) of all of our bodily proteins. But if we think of our genetic endowment as first being a system of consciousness as our reincarnational history is, we can see how the two nonphysical systems could be intermixed, as Seth put it, with one influencing the other. Conceivably, each of us could be a mixed bag of ancestral and reincarnational heritages, then--more "mongrelized" than we may care to admit. Interesting.... What we choose to do with those possibilities that we present ourselves with at each temporal birth may be another matter entirely.
quotes.translation: 我相信，在目前，研究人员不可能找到任何证据表明，转世遗产被编码在我们每个细胞核中46条染色体上排列的大约10万个基因中。我们说某个基因包含制造某种蛋白质的指令，例如，身体在眼睛的构建或功能中使用这种蛋白质，并且在表达该代码时，该基因传递从物理祖先继承的特征——但这种禀赋是否也以任何方式受到转世属性的影响或引导？那些因素是否可能与从祖父那里继承的一样强大？每个细胞中的基因在提供颤抖的模板（通过核酸DNA和信使RNA）来制造我们所有身体蛋白质方面，各有其个体工作。但如果我们首先将我们的基因禀赋视为一个意识系统，就像我们的转世历史一样，我们就可以看到这两个非物理系统如何能够混合在一起，正如赛斯所说，一个影响另一个。可以想象，我们每个人都可能是祖先和转世遗产的混合体——比我们可能愿意承认的更加“混血”。有趣的是……我们选择如何利用我们在每次时间出生时呈现给自己的那些可能性，可能完全是另一回事。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: I referred to a "successful" progression because reaching into the future is evidently much more difficult. By its very nature a future life cannot be proven--records checked, and so forth. Anything goes. Jane and I have read of many systems designed to regress the individual to past lives. Often such "trips" are mediated by hypnosis. It can even happen spontaneously, and I had a most exhilarating glimpse of a past life of my own that way. (See Session 721 in Volume 2 of "Unknown" Reality.) However, neither of us have had such an outright encounter with a future self--that we know of. I'd say that under hypnosis the urge to fantasize the future lives must be a tempting one; but what's the explanation for achieving little more than a formless future state while "under," no matter how hard one tries? The failure to get there, to turn time around, could be taken as a sign of resistance on the part of the present self. (Or even a past self or selves, but that's too complicated a subject to go into here.)
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 6: Genetic Heritage and Reincarnational Predilections

quotes.text: Now in a manner of speaking--though I see that little time has passed in this living room where I speak with Ruburt's permission--we have transcended time to some extent this evening, for in what I have said there are indeed hints and illusions--cadences--that can, if you are ready, give you a feeling for existence as it is outside of time's context. Even to try and verbally present such material necessitates alterations involving perception, for while that gridwork appears quite stable to your senses, giving you a reliable picture of reality, this is also because you have trained yourselves to pick up certain signals only. Others at other levels are (underlined) available. You can (underlined) tune into cellular consciousness, for example.
quotes.translation: 现在，从某种意义上说——尽管我看到在这个客厅里，我经鲁柏允许说话，时间几乎没有流逝——今晚我们在某种程度上超越了时间，因为在我所说的话中，确实有暗示和幻觉——韵律——如果你准备好了，它们能让你感受到时间背景之外的存在。即使试图口头呈现这样的材料，也需要涉及感知的改变，因为虽然那个网格在你的感官看来相当稳定，给你一幅可靠的现实图景，但这也是因为你训练自己只接收某些信号。其他层面的其他信号是（强调）可用的。例如，你（强调）可以调谐到细胞意识。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Those "properties" are the faculties of the imagination, creativity, telepathy, clairvoyance, and dreaming, as well as the functions of logic and reason. You know that you dream. You know that you think. Those are direct experiences. (Pause.) Anytime you use instruments to probe into the nature of reality, you are looking at a kind of secondary evidence, no matter how excellent the instruments may be. The subjective evidence of dreaming, for example, is far more "convincing" and irrefutable than is the evidence for an expanding universe, black holes, or even atoms and molecules themselves. Although instruments can indeed be most advantageous in many ways, they still present you with secondary rather than primary tools of investigation--and they distort the nature of reality far more than the subjective attributes of thoughts, feelings, and intuitions do.
quotes.translation: 那些“属性”是想象力、创造力、千里眼、预知力、做梦的能力，以及逻辑和理性的功能。你知道自己做梦。你知道自己思考。这些都是直接经验。（停顿。）每当你使用仪器探究实相的本质时，无论仪器多么精良，你看到的都是一种间接证据。例如，做梦的主观证据远比宇宙膨胀、黑洞甚至原子和分子本身的证据更“令人信服”且无可辩驳。虽然仪器在许多方面确实极为有利，但它们呈现给你的仍是次要而非首要的探究工具——而且它们对实相本质的扭曲程度，远大于思想、情感和直觉这些主观属性。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Religion was hampered--and is--by its own interpretation of good and evil, but it did not deny the existence of other versions of consciousness, or differing kinds of psychological activity and life. (Long pause.) Reincarnation suggests, of course, the extension of personal existence beyond one time period, independently of one bodily form, the translation or transmission of intelligence through nonphysical frameworks, and implies psychological behavior, memory and desire as purposeful action without the substance of any physical mechanism--propositions that science at its present stage of development simply could not buy, and for which it could find no evidence, for its methods would automatically preclude the type of experience that such evidence would require.
quotes.translation: 宗教一直受到——现在仍然受到——自身对善恶诠释的阻碍，但它并未否认其他意识版本或不同种类的心理活动与生命的存在。（长停顿。）轮回当然暗示了个人存在超越单一时间段的延伸，独立于单一身体形态，通过非物理架构传递或传输智能，并暗示心理行为、记忆和欲望是在没有任何物理机制实质的情况下进行的有目的行动——这些命题在科学目前的发展阶段根本无法接受，也找不到证据，因为科学的方法会自动排除这类证据所需的经验类型。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Jane doesn't agree with my doubts. As always, she's been letting me put this book together the way I think best--and inevitably that way has followed how we've been trying to understand our joint long-term situation. She innocently accepts my labors as they come out. And that trust always reflects, I'm sure, Seth's own larger view of reality, as I just quoted him from Session 915. Our challenges echo throughout all of our probable realities simultaneously, and through all of them together the largest picture of Jane and myself is presented. In this probable reality we work with what we can pick up from that great whole. We keep trying to learn to ask better questions.

quotes.text: "Violence will always be used creatively. You cannot be destructive even when you try. Beyond that, however, in the meantime the violence that you do, you do to yourself. You are a part of All That Is--of all the nature that you know and experience, of the world that you know, and even a part of the world that you know that you do not like. If you rip off the wing of a fly, you are yourself less. If you purposefully, now, or with malice, step upon an ant, then to the extent of your malice you step upon yourself all unknowing. Violence will always be used creatively, but if you do not understand this--and at your present rate of development you do not--then any violence is violence against yourself. This applies to each of you, for when you think in terms of violence you think in terms of malice or aggression. Despite all man does, he cannot really work any destruction--but while he believes in destruction, then to that extent he minimizes what he is, and must work harder to use creativity."
quotes.translation: “暴力永远会被创造性地使用。即使你试图破坏，也无法真正做到。然而除此之外，在此期间你所施加的暴力，都是施加于你自己。你是万有的一部分——是所有你所知所体验的自然的一部分，是你所知道的世界的一部分，甚至是你所知道但不喜欢的世界的一部分。如果你扯下苍蝇的翅膀，你自身就减少了。如果你现在故意或怀着恶意踩踏一只蚂蚁，那么你的恶意有多大，你就在不知不觉中踩踏了你自己。暴力永远会被创造性地使用，但如果你不理解这一点——以你们目前的发展速度，你们并不理解——那么任何暴力都是对自身的暴力。这对你们每个人都适用，因为当你们以暴力的方式思考时，你们就在以恶意或攻击的方式思考。尽管人类所做的一切，他实际上无法造成任何破坏——但当他相信破坏时，他就因此最小化了自己的本质，并且必须更努力地运用创造力。”
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: 2. Sometimes together, sometimes separately, Jane and I listen to portions of the tapes students had made of the sessions and other exciting happenings in her ESP classes. I'd hardly put into shape for Session 933 my musings about the workings of consciousness than I heard Seth come through with these two passages; at once they reminded me of what I'd just been writing about.

quotes.text: Company projections are that the entire cleanup at TMI won't be completed until the end of 1988--more than nine years after the accident took place. Current plans are that once the radioactive water is drained from the containment building of Unit No. 2, engineers will conduct remote investigations of the core of the reactor itself. A specially designed video camera will be inserted into the core so that the actual damage to the pencil-thin fuel rods can be assessed; and hundreds of thousands of sonar readings, taken through openings already present in the reactor, will be assembled by computer into images of the core. Several major steps must follow, all of them on an enormous scale: the lifting of the 160-ton metal "head," or cap, of the reactor; the removal of the upper plenum assembly, the 55-ton mechanism which makes possible the raising and lowering of fuel control rods into the 100-ton reactor core, thus regulating the intensity of its nuclear reactions; and eventually, the difficult piece-by-piece removal of the damaged core itself. Even then, the core will still be so radioactive that most of the work will have to be done by remote-controlled devices. Finally, the cavernous containment building itself will be cleaned, again by remote control.
quotes.translation: 公司预测，三里岛事故的整个清理工作要到1988年底才能完成——距离事故发生已超过九年。目前的计划是，一旦放射性水从二号机组的安全壳建筑中排干，工程师将对反应堆堆芯进行远程调查。一台专门设计的摄像机将被插入堆芯，以便评估铅笔粗细的燃料棒的实际损坏程度；通过反应堆现有开口获取的数十万次声纳读数，将由计算机组装成堆芯图像。随后必须进行几个主要步骤，所有步骤规模都极为庞大：吊起反应堆160吨重的金属“顶盖”；移除上腔室组件——这个55吨重的机构负责将燃料控制棒升降至100吨重的反应堆堆芯，从而调节核反应强度；最终，逐步移除受损的堆芯本身。即便如此，堆芯的放射性仍将极强，大部分工作必须由遥控设备完成。最后，巨大的安全壳建筑本身也将通过遥控方式清理。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: I am speaking now for your convenience simply. There are four cornerstone personalities involved in your seminar. Now three of these personalities are yours: one is not yours. One is not"yours" in your terms as of this time. When you look up and recognize, you will accept that which you do not now recognize as yourself, as yourself. If you looked up now, you would not recognize the self that you will recognize. Because the Spacious present exists as it is, the recognition has already been made. You have not yet caught up with it, you see. You are slowpokes. My friend Ruburt in many respects is also a slowpoke. The environment is not where you think it is. I refer to these seminars. The voice that does not"speak" in your terms is yours, but the instructor is not yourself as you know yourself. It is a self you do not as yet know in your terms.
quotes.translation: 我现在为了方便你们理解而说话。你们的工作坊涉及四个基石人格。其中三个是你们自己的：一个不是你们的。一个在你们目前的理解中不是“你们的”。当你们抬头认出时，你们将接受那个现在不被你们认作自己的自己。如果你们现在抬头，你们不会认出那个你们将要认出的自己。因为广袤的当下如其本然地存在，这份认出已经完成。你们还没有赶上它，明白吗？你们是慢吞吞的家伙。我的朋友鲁柏在很多方面也是个慢吞吞的家伙。环境并不在你们以为的地方。我指的是这些工作坊。那个在你们理解中不“说话”的声音是你们的，但指导者并非你们所知的自己。它是一个你们在目前理解中尚未知晓的自己。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: You are all pieces, you see, of the whole; but you are not all the same pieces of the whole, but rather individualized pieces of the whole. You do not all fit together like a crossword puzzle that any idiot can put together. You are still highly individualized portions of the whole. You are the whole, but you are all highly unique. You fit into different portions of the whole. The self or structure or personality travels outward and inward and (if you will forgive me) in all directions. It is action. It constantly changes. Each self as you know it has its own abilities and inclinations and sympathies. It has its own particular place within the Pyramid Gestalt. It can contact that whole self which in your terms does not yet exist, but which is of course always present. In your search you must contact that whole portion of yourself toward which you are growing--toward which I hope you are growing. This is your individual circuit, so to speak. All ways are one way, but your way is your own way. And you can travel no other. He is--Baba--highly advanced indeed. He is a way, however; he is not the end. He is not completed. He is right, but he is wrong in taking pleasure in his rightness. (I have never been known for my own Humilitye. It ill-behooves me to speak.) Nevertheless he who is and knows that he is, is. He has no need for words and he has no need to proclaim himself, for he speaks without the necessity for words and he is heard. Those who are really heard have no need for words. I speak to you now in words because without words now, there would not be the necessary understanding that must be reached before I can become wordless.
quotes.translation: 你们都是整体的碎片，明白吗？但你们并非整体中相同的碎片，而是整体中个体化的碎片。你们不会像任何傻瓜都能拼好的填字游戏那样拼合在一起。你们仍然是整体中高度个体化的部分。你们是整体，但你们都是高度独特的。你们融入整体的不同部分。自我或结构或人格向外向内（请原谅我这么说）向所有方向移动。它是行动。它不断变化。你们所知的每个自我都有其自身的能力、倾向和共鸣。它在金字塔完形中有其特定的位置。它可以接触那个在你们理解中尚未存在、但当然始终在场的整体自我。在你们的探索中，你们必须接触那个你们正在向其成长的整体自我部分——我希望你们正在向其成长。这可以说是你们各自的回路。所有道路都是一条路，但你们的路是你们自己的路。你们无法走其他路。他——巴巴——确实高度进化。然而，他是一条路，不是终点。他尚未完成。他是对的，但他以自己正确为乐却是错的。（我从不以谦逊著称。由我来说这话并不合适。）然而，那个存在并知道自己存在者，就是存在。他无需言语，也无需宣告自己，因为他无需言语就能说话，且被听见。那些真正被听见者无需言语。我现在用言语对你们说话，因为如果没有言语，就无法达成在我能变得无言之前必须达到的必要理解。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: There is nothing wrong in trying to perceive tomorrow. (Pause.) It is risky however to live today in such a manner. There are too many errors that can be made when dealing with precognitive elements. You are dealing with a world of probabilities. Now Ferd looked into a possible future, and this was quite legitimate--as a probable future. There is much concerning time that you do not yet understand, and I cannot explain it to you, unfortunately, in an evening. This may sound contradictory, but it is not. It is possible to perceive the future as it will be; on the other hand the future itself is always changing, for you change it in the present. (Pause.) In the precise moment in which you spoke the words, there was a probability, and a good one, that the event would occur as stated.
quotes.translation: 试图预知明日并无不妥。（停顿）然而以这种方式活在当下却充满风险。处理预知要素时极易出错。你们面对的是概率的世界。弗德窥见了一个可能的未来——作为概率性未来而言，这完全合理。关于时间你们尚有许多不解之处，遗憾的是我无法在一夜之间向你们阐明。这听似矛盾，实则不然。既可能预知未来的实相，而未来本身又始终在变化——因为你们在当下改变着它。（停顿）就在你们说出那些话语的精确时刻，存在着一个概率——且是相当高的概率——事件会如所述般发生。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: Now I am only speaking because many of you have known that I was here. And it ill behooves me not to bid you welcome when you felt my presence. But in many of your discussions, there has only been surface thought and surface feelings... and we do not walk on the surface in this class. I would push your heads collectively and individually underneath... for you still do not understand that you create your physical reality! you form your blocks and shove them up upon the surface of the earth. You cannot solve the problems of your world on a surface level. If you could, you would have done it centuries ago. You must step out of your present perspective in order to see your world clearly. Here you are not to hide within the closet of 3 dimensionality and cry, "It is dark and I cannot see!" And refuse to use the inner light that can alone aid your vision.
quotes.translation: 此刻我之所以发言，是因为你们许多人已知我在此。当你们感知到我的临在时，我若不以欢迎回应便不合时宜。但你们诸多讨论中，始终只有表层思维与表层感受……而本课程从不浮于表面。我要将你们的头颅——集体与个体——按入深处……因你们仍未明白自己创造着物质实相！你们垒起砖块，将其堆砌在地表之上。你们无法在表层解决世界的问题。若能如此，几个世纪前便已达成。唯有跳出当下视角，方能清晰看见世界。在此你们不可躲进三次元的壁橱哭喊："太暗了，我看不见！"却拒绝运用那唯一能助你视物的内在之光。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: The portrait is of Joseph and Ruburt. These are not portraits of yourselves as past personalities in your terms, or of particular re-incarnational selves. They are pictorial representatives of the whole selves that you are. In your terms the selves that are the sum of your re-incarnational personalities. These whole selves then are a part of your entity. Your probable selves are also a part of your entity, however. If you want to think in terms of guides, in quotes "angels," then you have the reality behind those terms. Ruburt and Joseph are the names I use for you, for these names themselves imply your greater goals. These personalities are aware of your present existence, and give you advice when you are in the sleep state. They are a source of strength. You can draw upon their knowledge, and you can also draw upon the knowledge of your own re-incarnational selves, past, in quotes, and future.
quotes.translation: 这幅肖像画的是约瑟夫与鲁柏。按你们的说法，这并非你们过去世人格或特定转世自我的画像。它们是你们完整本体的图像化表征——按你们的说法，即累世人格总和之自我。这些完整本体是你们存有的一部分。然而你们的概率自我同样属于存有。若想以"指导灵"或带引号的"天使"来理解，那么这些词汇背后确实存在实相。鲁柏与约瑟夫是我为你们所用的名字，因这些名字本身暗示着你们更宏大的目标。这些人格知晓你们当下的存在，并在睡眠状态给予建议。它们是力量的源泉。你们可汲取其知识，亦可汲取自身转世自我——带引号的"过去"与"未来"——的知识。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: An individual will therefore feel more at home operating within certain frequencies, and he will feel less at home with others. The original thought is used as a pattern, therefore, for the creation of a new electrical reality, which may or may not be directed at any given receiver. It is obvious that the attempt to duplicate is present; and were it not for this attempt to duplicate, then there would be little similarity between any separate identities.
quotes.translation: 因此个体在特定频率中运作时会倍感自在，而在其他频率中则觉不适。原始思想被用作模式，用以创造新的电性实相——这实相可能指向也可能不指向特定接收者。显然复制意图始终存在；若非这种复制企图，各独立身份之间将毫无相似之处。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: It constructs ideas instead within another electrical system. Yet because of the nature of the personality, no dream exists in a vacuum, and every dream is recorded by the inner self. I am making an attempt here this evening, since we have three present, to give a very brief, and I am afraid inadequate explanation, that will however serve as a basis so that these three, at least, will be able to have some sort of a standing ground for other discussions.
quotes.translation: 它反而在另一电性系统中构建思想。然而由于人格的本质，没有梦境存在于真空之中，每个梦都被内在自我记录。今晚我试图——因有三位在场——给予极为简短且恐不充分的解释，但这至少能为这三位提供基础，使其在后续讨论中有所依凭。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Concentrating upon your own camouflage universe, you are able to distinguish only the distortive pattern, and from this pattern you deduce your ideas of cause and effect, past, present and future, and ideas of an expanding universe that bloats. Consciousness takes up no space nor is it enclosed by time, as you know time. The camouflage patterns that seem to enclose consciousness are temporary, for short term only, and for limited but necessary purpose.
quotes.translation: 专注于你们自己的伪装宇宙时，你们只能辨识扭曲模式，并由此推导出因果、过去、现在与未来的概念，以及膨胀宇宙的荒谬观念。意识不占据空间，亦不受限于你们所认知的时间。那些看似包裹意识的伪装模式只是暂时的——仅为短期及有限却必要的目标而存在。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: It is very difficult for them to understand your ideas of past, present and future. Now give us a moment. (No real pause.) In other dimensions, or if you prefer at other stages of development, the multidimensional personality is aware of its primary identity, and is also aware simultaneously of personality offshoots that it has sent into many realities, into probable systems, as it pursues all of the probable acts and creations inherent in its nature.
quotes.translation: 他们很难理解你们对过去、现在和未来的概念。现在请稍等片刻。（没有真正的停顿。）在其他维度，或者如果你更喜欢用其他发展阶段来说，多重人格体知晓其本体身份，同时也同时知晓它送入许多实相、可能系统的分支人格，因为它追求其本性中固有的所有可能行为和创造。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: The species chooses the best conditions in which to display and develop such a capacity to the utmost, taking into consideration all its other needs and purposes. The particular, brilliant, intensified flowering of painting and sculpture that took place, say, in the time of Michelangelo (1475-1564) could not, in your probability, have occurred after the birth of technology, for example, and certainly not in your own era, where images are flashed constantly before your eyes on television and in the movies, where they are rambunctiously present in your magazines and advertisements. You are everywhere surrounded by photography of all kinds, but in those days images outside of those provided by nature's objects were highly rare.
quotes.translation: 物种选择最佳条件来最大限度地展现和发展这种能力，同时考虑其所有其他需求和目的。例如，在米开朗基罗（1475-1564）时代发生的绘画和雕塑的特别辉煌、密集的绽放，在你们的概率中，不可能发生在科技诞生之后，当然也不可能发生在你们自己的时代——在这个时代，影像不断在电视和电影中闪过你们的眼前，在杂志和广告中喧闹地呈现。你们到处被各种摄影包围，但在那些日子里，除了自然物体提供的影像之外，影像极为罕见。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now if you are looking for simple definitions to explain the psyche, I will be of no help. If you want to experience the splendid creativity of your own being, however, then I will use methods that will arouse your greatest adventuresomeness, your boldest faith in yourself, and I will paint pictures of your psyche that will lead you to experience even its broadest reaches, if you so desire. The psyche, then, is not a known land. It is not simply an alien land, to which or through which you can travel. It is not a completed or nearly complete subjective universe already there for you to explore. It is, instead, an ever-forming state of being, in which your present sense of existence resides. You create it and it creates you.
quotes.translation: 现在，如果你在寻找简单的定义来解释心灵，我帮不上忙。然而，如果你想体验自己存在的辉煌创造力，那么我将使用能激发你最伟大冒险精神、最勇敢自信的方法，我会描绘你心灵的图景，引导你体验其最广阔的领域——如果你愿意的话。因此，心灵不是已知的土地。它不只是一片你可以前往或穿越的异域。它不是已经存在、等待你去探索的完整或近乎完整的主观宇宙。相反，它是一个不断形成的存在状态，你当前的存在感就居于其中。你创造它，它也创造你。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: There are a few points that I would like to make of a general nature. Ruburt has been reading Jung, though not consistently. The libido does not originate in the individual subconscious of the present personality. It originates instead in the energy of the entity and inner self, and is directed by means of the inner senses, outward so to speak, through the deeper layers of the individual subconscious mind, then through the outer or personal layers.
quotes.translation: 我想提出一些一般性的观点。鲁柏一直在阅读荣格，虽然并不持续。力比多并非源自当前人格的个人潜意识。相反，它源自存有和内我的能量，并通过内在感官，可以说是向外，经由个人潜意识的深层，然后通过外层或个人层面来引导。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: All of these issues prepared you psychically and psychologically to make this kind of contact. The balance between aggressiveness and passivity on Ruburt's part also forms a highly charged medium that allows him to act in his present capacity in the sessions. Any exploration into other realities involves an aggressive thrust on the one hand toward fulfillment, with hope of freedom you see. This has its psychological connotations within your system.
quotes.translation: 所有这些议题在心理和精神上为你做好了进行这种接触的准备。鲁柏身上攻击性与被动性之间的平衡也形成了一个高度充电的媒介，使他能够在课程中以当前的身份行事。任何对其他实相的探索都涉及一方面朝向圆满的攻击性推进，伴随着对自由的希望，你看。这在你们的系统内有其心理含义。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: The gradations of intensities are so minute that it would be impossible to measure them, and yet each field contains in coded form the actual living reality of endless eons; contains therefore what you would call the past, present and future of unnumbered universes; contains the actual coded data of any and every consciousness that has been or will be, in any universe; those that have appeared to vanish, and those which seemingly do not yet exist.
quotes.translation: 强度的层次如此细微，以至于无法测量，然而每个场域都以编码形式包含了无尽永劫的实际活生生的实相；因此包含了你们所谓的无数宇宙的过去、现在和未来；包含了任何宇宙中曾经存在或将要存在的每一个意识的实际编码数据；那些看似已经消失的，以及那些看似尚未存在的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: （From the sketch on page 250 it can be seen that parallel lines, both horizontal and tall, or vertical, are present; these are the customary wavy lines of cancellation. See stamps numbered 1, 2, and 3 on the sketch. On the envelope paper beneath stamp #2 is a green printed box of the type used for prepaid mail, but I do not know if this is what Seth means by "lines on the other side. " This could also refer to the letters the stamps once serviced.
quotes.translation: （从第250页的草图可以看出，存在平行线，既有水平的也有垂直的；这些是常见的波浪形注销线。参见草图上编号为1、2、3的邮票。在2号邮票下方的信封纸上，有一个用于预付邮资的绿色印刷方框，但我不确定这是否就是赛斯所说的"另一面的线条"。这也可能指这些邮票曾经服务过的信件。）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: （In the 54th session Seth went into considerable detail in explaining the construction of entities. He could not give us this information earlier, he said, because we would have leaped to the conclusion that he was Ruburt's [Janes] subconscious mind; this is not so. As everything else does, entities constantly change. To quote Seth: "Ruburt is not myself now, in his present life; he is nevertheless an extension and materialization of the Seth that I was at one time... He grew, evolved and expanded in terms of a particular, personal set of value fulfillments... He is now an actual gestalt, a personality that was one of the probable personalities into which Seth could grow. I represent another, I am another. "
quotes.translation: （在第54节课中，赛斯详细解释了存有的构造。他说，他之前无法告诉我们这些信息，是因为我们会草率地得出结论，认为他是鲁柏（珍）的潜意识；事实并非如此。如同其他一切事物，存有也在不断变化。引用赛斯的话："鲁柏在他现在的这一生中并非现在的我；但他是我曾经是的那个赛斯的一种延伸和物质化……他根据一套特定的、个人化的价值完成而成长、演化并扩展……他现在是一个实际完形，一个曾是赛斯可能发展成的可能人格之一。我代表另一个，我是另一个。"）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Certain kinds of pain automatically eject consciousness from the body. Such pain cannot be verbalized, for it is a mixture of pain and pleasure, a tearing free, and it automatically brings about an almost exhilarating release of consciousness. Such pain is also very brief. Under your present system, however, drugs are usually administered, in which case pain is somewhat minimized, but prolonged -- not triggering the natural release mechanisms.
quotes.translation: 某些类型的痛苦会自动将意识逐出身体。这种痛苦无法用言语表达，因为它是痛苦与快乐的混合，一种撕裂般的解放，会自动带来一种近乎狂喜的意识释放。这种痛苦也极为短暂。然而，在你们目前的体系下，通常会使用药物，这样痛苦会稍微减轻，但会延长——从而不会触发自然的释放机制。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In an inverted time system the momentum is recognized and it is also taken advantage of, in that it is utilized by individual consciousness, so that your so-called present, past and future can be viewed as existing in a spacious now. Again, this sort of a system is very close to the true nature of time.
quotes.translation: 在倒转的时间系统中，动量被识别并被利用，因为它被个体意识所运用，因此你们所谓的现在、过去和未来可以被视为存在于一个广袤的当下。再次强调，这种系统非常接近时间的真实本质。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: And even if you live in a desert, you take it on faith that there are indeed great cultivated fields and torrents of rain. It is true that some of your faith is based on knowledge. Others have traveled where you have not, and television provides you with images. Despite this, however, your senses present you with only a picture of your immediate environment, unless they are cultivated in certain particular manners that are relatively unusual.
quotes.translation: 即使你生活在沙漠中，你也凭信心相信确实存在广阔的耕地和倾盆大雨。诚然，你的部分信心基于知识。别人去过你没去过的地方，电视为你提供了影像。尽管如此，你的感官只向你呈现你直接环境的画面，除非它们以某些相对不寻常的特定方式被培养。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: As a rule you have enough difficulty dealing with the day's occurrences, much less next week's, and so in the sequence of events the reality of probable actions is usually hidden from your view. (Pause.) This more complex reality is an ever-existing property of your personal creaturehood. Beside this, in your terms you exist as a creature more than once. In each of your "reincarnational" existences you are faced with the same relationship with probabilities. In each case, also, the nature of the conscious mind sets up its own territory-of-identity (with hyphens) that it regards as its own. This provides a clear focus in which "present" action can be considered. These incarnations are all simultaneous.
quotes.translation: 通常，你处理当天发生的事情已经够困难了，更不用说下周的事了，因此在事件序列中，可能行动的真实性通常被隐藏在你的视野之外。（停顿。）这种更复杂的实相是你个人存有的一种永恒存在的属性。除此之外，从你的角度来看，你作为存有存在不止一次。在你每一次"转世"存在中，你都面临着与可能性的相同关系。在每种情况下，意识心的本质也会建立它自己视为己有的身份领域（带连字符）。这提供了一个清晰的焦点，在其中可以考虑"当下"的行动。这些转世都是同时发生的。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: "In your terms -- the phrase is necessary -- the moment point, the present, is the point of interaction between all existences and reality. All probabilities flow through it, though one of your moment points may be experienced as centuries, or as a breath, in other probable realities of which you are a part." Thus, just as a moment point can be explored indefinitely, so each newly arising question results in an ever-widening pool of inquiry.
quotes.translation: “用你们的话来说——这个措辞是必要的——当下点，即现在，是所有存在与实相之间的交互点。所有可能性都流经它，尽管你们的一个当下点可能在你们所参与的其他可能性实相中被体验为几个世纪，或一次呼吸。”因此，正如一个当下点可以被无限探索，每个新出现的问题都会导致一个不断扩大的探究之池。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: To me, some of the most important material in Volume 1 is Jane's information on her sensing of other neurological pulses as they're connected with probable events, and how she picked up those pulses by bypassing her direct, or ordinary neurological impact. See her work in appendixes 4 and 5. Seth also discussed such neurological changeovers in Session 685, among others. I think this kind of material offers a rich source for future scientific investigation.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: In other words, a dream allows the inner self to view itself within the spacious present. Now, chemically the physical body does need to dream. That is, dreaming is a necessity if the physical body is to survive. This is the result of certain chemical reactions and chemical necessities, chemical excesses that build up during the days, inciting the mental dream mechanism.
quotes.translation: 换句话说，梦境让内在自体在广袤的现在中观照自身。现在，从化学角度来说，肉体确实需要做梦。也就是说，如果肉体要存活，做梦是必要的。这是某些化学反应和化学需求的结果，是白天积累的化学过剩物，激发了心理的梦境机制。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: In denying life to yourself you end up by denying life to others. Now I wish this session was recorded so you could hear me, for I hold you, as Ruburt does, closely and dearly as a friend. But you are bound to misinterpret what I say in some sessions, and so it behooves me to speak more plainly. You do not realize now, you do not let yourself realize, the beauty and the complicated reality of your being. You do not let yourself realize the spontaneity and joyful burst that is your inner self, that results in this present human personality that you call yourself. Nor the effort and creative energy that has gone into your making, and that sings within your being like the first morning of creation.
quotes.translation: 否定自己的生命，最终会导致否定他人的生命。现在我希望这次课程被录下来，这样你就能听到我的声音，因为我像鲁柏一样，把你当作亲密而珍贵的朋友。但你在某些课程中必然会误解我说的话，因此我更应该说得更直白些。你现在没有意识到，也不让自己意识到，你存在的美丽与复杂的实相。你没有让自己意识到那种自发性与喜悦的迸发，那是你的内在自体，它导致了你现在称之为自己的这个当前人格。你也没有意识到投入你形成过程中的努力与创造力，它像创世的第一天早晨一样在你的存在中歌唱。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: The division between the two aspects of experience begins to take on the characteristics of completely diverse behavior. The unconscious becomes more and more unfamiliar to consciousness. Those beliefs build up about it, and the symbolisms involved are exaggerated. The unknown seems to be threatening and degenerate. The color black assumes stronger tendencies in its connection with evil -- something to be avoided. Self-annihilation seems to be a threat ever-present in the dream or sleep state. At the same time all of those flamboyant, creative, spontaneous, emotional surges that emerge normally from the unconscious become feared and projected outward, then, upon enemies, other races and creeds.
quotes.translation: 经验的两个方面之间的分裂开始呈现出完全不同的行为特征。无意识对意识来说变得越来越陌生。关于它的那些信念逐渐形成，所涉及的象征被夸大。未知似乎具有威胁性且堕落。黑色在与邪恶的联系中呈现出更强的倾向——某种需要避免的东西。自我湮灭似乎是在梦境或睡眠状态中始终存在的威胁。同时，所有那些从无意识中正常涌现的华丽、创造性、自发、情感性的冲动都变得令人恐惧，并被投射到外部，然后投射到敌人、其他种族和信仰上。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Think of the greater you -- call it the entity if you want to -- as forming a psychic structure quite as real as your physical one, but composed of many selves. As each cell of your body has its position within your corporeal space and boundaries, so each self within the entity is aware of its own "time" and dimension of activity. The body is a temporal structure. The cells, however, while a part of this body, are not aware of the entire dimension in which your consciousness dwells. They do not perceive all of the elements that are available even in three-dimensional experience, yet your present consciousness -- seemingly so much more sophisticated -- physically rests upon cellular awareness.
quotes.translation: 想想那个更大的你——如果你愿意，可以称之为存有——它形成了一个与你的肉体同样真实的心理结构，但由许多自体组成。正如你身体的每个细胞在你肉体空间和边界内都有其位置，存有中的每个自体也意识到自己的“时间”和活动维度。身体是一个时间结构。然而，细胞虽然是这个身体的一部分，却并不意识到你的意识所栖居的整个维度。它们甚至无法感知三维经验中可用的所有元素，而你当前的意识——看似复杂得多——在物理上却依赖于细胞的觉知。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: (12:02.) In a very limited and fumbling manner this is hinted at through the use of computers, where you try to assess "future probabilities" and act accordingly in your present. The mind can do this far better than any computer. If it believed this, then certain portions of the brain would be activated. The brain would become aware of more of the mind's knowledge, and the probabilities of future events would be made consciously available.
quotes.translation: （12:02。）通过使用计算机，以一种非常有限且笨拙的方式暗示了这一点，你们试图评估“未来的可能性”并在当下据此行动。心灵比任何计算机都能更好地做到这一点。如果它相信这一点，那么大脑的某些部分就会被激活。大脑会意识到更多心灵的知识，未来事件的概率就会有意识地可用。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: In your terms, probabilities are extensions and variations on the growth principle that is quite obvious in your daily reality. Such growth is a natural manifestation flourishing within your particular area of actuality, observable to your senses. Again, other entirely natural manifestations of that principle exist. Some can only be glimpsed in distorted form because of other "natural" conditions that you cannot perceive. Probabilities involve you with a rich psychological growth and development, present but not observable in your "home ground." Any kind of existence happens within the context of nature, and nature includes the soul. Your definition of nature has simply been too limiting.
quotes.translation: 用你们的话来说，概率是成长原则的延伸与变奏，这在你们的日常实相中显而易见。这种成长是自然的表现，在你们特定的实相领域中蓬勃发展，感官可察。再次强调，该原则还存在其他完全自然的表现形式。有些只能以扭曲形态被瞥见，因为存在你们无法感知的其他"自然"条件。概率让你们参与丰富的心理成长与发展，这在你们的"主场"中虽存在却不可观察。任何存在形式都发生在自然的脉络中，而自然包含灵魂。你们对自然的定义实在太过局限了。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Our next law of the inner universe is the capacity for infinite mobility, this occurring within the spacious present, which is an infinite spacious present.
quotes.translation: 我们下一个内在宇宙法则是无限移动的能力，这发生在广袤的当下之中，那是一个无限的广袤当下。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: Creation, therefore, constantly continues, and not always along the lines of old patterns, but by means of completely different patterns. And another small but interesting point: On your own plane, there is a subconscious storehouse of knowledge, whereby it is known in a condensed fashion, by all molecules and atoms, exactly which variant or evolutionary attempts have been made, with what results--and always with an eye out, so to speak, for circumstances that might fit forms once adopted with failure, or to attempt other forms for which present circumstances may not be right. I have said that, first of all, molecules and atoms and even smaller particles, have a condensed consciousness.
quotes.translation: 因此，创造持续不断进行，并非总沿袭旧模式，而是通过完全不同的模式。另一个有趣的小要点：在你们自己的层面上，存在一个潜意识知识库，所有分子和原子都以浓缩方式知晓，究竟尝试过哪些变体或进化尝试，结果如何——而且始终留意着，可以说，寻找可能适合那些曾失败过的形态的环境，或尝试当前环境可能不适合的其他形态。我曾说过，首先，分子、原子乃至更小的粒子都具有浓缩的意识。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: There are various types of dreams and dream fragments. I will follow this through also later, since in these beginning sessions I am giving you what may be considered a broad outline to be filled in. These dissociated states often occur in sleep, an excellent time since the ego is quieted. At such times it is extremely possible for present personalities to be visited by others such as myself, but only on the bidding of the entity itself.
quotes.translation: 存在各种类型的梦境和梦的片段。我稍后也会跟进这一点，因为在最初的这些课程中，我给你们的是可视为待填充的粗略大纲。这些解离状态常发生在睡眠中，那是极佳时机，因为自我已安静下来。此时，当前人格极有可能被像我这样的其他存在拜访，但仅在存有本身的要求下进行。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Your discipline in working habits and in living habits is admirable. Through your past life, that is the life before this one, you progressed along these lines. You do not have to forge them with steel now. They are sufficiently strong and dependable. You do not have to feel that they will let you down. Now for your own sake and for the sake of the art that you follow, you must allow yourself more inner freedom. There is a tendency in your present personality for forging discipline into a bond that could tie down your strong creative tendencies. It is true you needed controls. Now you have them. You stamped down upon your impulses with a frenzy, since once you felt they betrayed you.
quotes.translation: 你在工作习惯和生活习惯上的自律令人钦佩。通过你的前世，即这一世之前的那一世，你沿着这些路线进步。你现在不必用钢铁去锻造它们。它们已足够坚固可靠。你不必觉得它们会让你失望。现在，为了你自己和你所追随的艺术，你必须允许自己拥有更多内在自由。在你当前人格中，有一种倾向，将自律锻造为可能束缚你强烈创造倾向的枷锁。确实，你曾需要控制。现在你已拥有它们。你曾疯狂压制自己的冲动，因为一旦你觉得它们背叛了你。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: I do not mean to imply, however, that only the great share in this communication of consciousness. (Pause.) A great simplicity is necessary, and out of this, many of the most lowly in men's terms also share in these communications. There is an unending conversation going on throughout the universe, and a most meaningful one. (Long pause.) Those from both your past and your future have a hand in your present world, and at this level the problems that have been met and will be encountered are being discussed. This is the heart of communication. It is most usually encountered either in a protected deep level of sleep or in a sudden spontaneous trance state. Great energy is generated.
quotes.translation: 然而，我并非暗示只有伟大者才参与这种意识交流。（停顿。）极大的单纯是必要的，由此，以人类标准而言最卑微的许多人也参与这些交流。整个宇宙中存在着无休止的对话，且是最有意义的对话。（长停顿。）来自你们过去和未来的存在都参与你们当前的世界，在这个层面上，已遭遇和将遇到的问题正在被讨论。这是交流的核心。它最常在受保护的深层睡眠状态或突然的自发恍惚状态中被遇到。巨大的能量被生成。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: I risk repeating myself, but I want these steps to be plain. This vital data is sent to the mind by the inner senses. Any material that is important for the body's contact with outer camouflage patterns is given to the brain. The subconscious, so-called, is a connective between mind and brain, between the inner senses and the outer senses. It is actually partly on your plane and partly on other planes. Portions of it do deal with camouflage patterns, with the personal past of the present personality, with racial camouflage memories; and the greater portion belongs to the inner world, and as data comes into it from the inner world, so can it reach far into the inner world itself.
quotes.translation: 我不怕重复，但希望这些步骤清晰明了。这些关键数据由内在感官传递给心智。任何对肉体与外在伪装模式接触重要的材料都会被输送给大脑。所谓的潜意识是心智与大脑、内在感官与外在感官之间的连接。它实际上部分存在于你们的层面，部分存在于其他层面。它的一部分确实处理伪装模式、当前人格的个人过去、种族伪装记忆；而更大部分则属于内在世界，当数据从内在世界进入时，它也能深入内在世界本身。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: The capacity and mechanism necessary to project idea into physical matter is, therefore, present in the individual atom and molecule. I mentioned how the atoms and molecules combine to form cells, while each individual atom and molecule does not give up its own uniqueness, but forms rather a gestalt, so that a cell is indeed a psychic gestalt given physical construction through the capacity that exists within each individual component.
quotes.translation: 因此，将意念投射为物质形态所需的能力与机制，存在于每个原子和分子之中。我曾提到原子和分子如何结合形成细胞，而每个原子和分子并未放弃其独特性，反而形成一种完形，因此细胞确实是一种心灵完形，通过每个组成部分内在的能力获得物质结构。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: I realize that such surface help at times could be most desirable. Basically however in your case the illness was not serious, and the advantage that you would derive from my help in a healing capacity would have been outweighed entirely by several disadvantages that are almost always present, in the case of healing that does not originate from inner comprehension.
quotes.translation: 我明白这种表面上的帮助有时非常可取。但基本上，在你的案例中，疾病并不严重，而我从疗愈能力上提供帮助所能带来的好处，完全会被几乎总是存在的几个弊端所抵消——当疗愈并非源于内在理解时。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: With the exception of the ego, the various parts of the personality do not react to time as a series of moments. All is experienced as present. The child therefore within the adult personality is not dead, nor are his reactions considered, basically, as reactions which are part of a past behavior pattern; but these reactions exist side by side with adult reactions.
quotes.translation: 除了自我之外，人格的各个部分并不将时间视为一系列时刻。一切都被体验为当下。因此，成人人格中的孩童并未死去，他的反应基本上也不被视为过去行为模式的一部分；这些反应与成人反应并存。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Other animals are kept out. The hunted and the prey are highly regulated. All areas of animal behavior alter to fit the circumstances as much as possible, and this includes sexual activity. To some extent the animals have been conditioned to the changing world. Now man is obviously part of nature, so you may say: "But those changes wrought by him are natural." When he studies such animal behavior, however, and sometimes uses the sexual patterns of the animals to make certain points about human sexuality, then man does not take this into consideration, but speaks as if the present observed animal behavior is the indication of a prime or basic nature inherent in their biology.
quotes.translation: 其他动物被排除在外。被捕食者与捕食者受到高度调控。动物行为的各个领域都尽可能根据环境变化而调整，这包括性活动。在某种程度上，动物已被调节以适应变化的世界。人类显然是自然的一部分，所以你们可能会说：“但他带来的这些变化是自然的。”然而，当人类研究动物行为，有时用动物的性模式来论证人类性行为的某些观点时，人类并未考虑这一点，而是仿佛当前观察到的动物行为是其生物学中原始或基本天性的指示。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Emotions take up a certain amount of what we call psychic space within the personality's awareness. As the intensity of the emotion fades, so it seems to you that it recedes in time. But this is an illusion caused by the limitations of your outer senses. Now. In the dream state the outer senses are to a large degree restrained in their activity. Therefore the dream state more clearly represents the actual nature of time. Intense emotions within the dream state are experienced as present time, and the personality moves easily through these emotional intensities without experiencing the sense of passing time, although any given dream may contain within it its own time element.
quotes.translation: 情绪在人格意识中占据一定量的我们称之为心灵空间的东西。随着情绪强度减弱，在你们看来它似乎在时间中消退。但这是由你们外在感官的局限性造成的幻觉。现在。在梦境状态中，外在感官的活动在很大程度上受到抑制。因此，梦境状态更清晰地代表了时间的真实本质。梦境状态中强烈的情绪被体验为当下，人格在这些情绪强度中轻松移动，而不会体验到时间流逝的感觉，尽管任何特定梦境都可能包含其自身的时间元素。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

source_raw.title: （Tracing of the photographic negative used in the 7th envelope test, in Session 187, September 13,1965.）

quotes.text: No one begins a race with a handicap, you may say, but that is obviously not the case. Individuals have often chosen such situations precisely as incentives, and many great men have done so. This does not mean that such disabilities are necessary. At any point that an individual realizes his point of power in the present, he will not need a barrier to test himself against, or to focus him in what he thinks of as the proper direction.
quotes.translation: 你可能会说，没有人会带着障碍开始赛跑，但显然事实并非如此。个体常常特意选择这样的处境作为激励，许多伟人正是如此。这并不意味着这些障碍是必要的。当一个人意识到自己在当下的力量点时，他就不再需要障碍来考验自己，或将他聚焦于自认为正确的方向。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

related_concepts.name_en: The point of power is in the present

quotes.text: It is energy concentrated to a degree quite unbelievable to you. It contains potentials unlimited, but it must work out its own identity and form its own worlds. It carries within it the burden of all being. Within it are personality potentials beyond your comprehension. Remember, this is your own soul or entity I am speaking of, as well as soul or entity in general. You are one manifestation of your own soul. How many of you would want to limit your reality, your entire reality, to the experience you now know? You do this when you imagine that your present self is your entire personality, or insist that your identity be maintained unchanged through an endless eternity.
quotes.translation: 它凝聚的能量程度对你们而言简直难以置信。它包含无限的潜能，但必须发展出自身的身份并形成自己的世界。它承载着所有存在的重担。其中蕴含着你们无法理解的人格潜能。请记住，我所说的正是你们自己的灵魂或存有，也是泛指的灵魂或存有。你们是自己灵魂的一种显现。你们当中有多少人愿意将自己的实相——全部的实相——局限于此刻所知的体验？当你们想象当下的自我就是完整的人格，或坚持自己的身份必须永恒不变地维持下去时，你们就在这样做。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Very vaguely indeed, this could be compared to a voice from one station suddenly being captured by another. There is much more here, almost impossible at present to explain. There are, from your standpoint, minutely sized systems that within themselves are infinite. You could be drawn into an entirely different value fulfillment system. All of this is very improbable, but it is possible and under drugs such experiences could result.
quotes.translation: 确实非常模糊，这可以比作一个电台的声音突然被另一个电台截获。这其中还有更多内容，目前几乎无法解释。从你们的角度来看，存在着一些微小到极致的系统，它们自身却是无限的。你们可能会被吸引进入一个完全不同的价值完成系统。这一切虽然极不可能，但却是可能的，在药物作用下可能会产生这类体验。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: Now, there is the impression of a new account. I am not certain as to what this refers to, but put it into the record. There will also be an improving of company prestige, and this development hinges, believe it or not, one man who is at present considered relatively innocuous and unimportant. It will be in the nature of a new scientific development. A rise of stock, a good rise for a three year period, a levelling off, and a drop.
quotes.translation: 现在，有一个新账户的印象。我不确定这指的是什么，但请将其记录在案。公司声望也将得到提升，而这一发展——信不信由你——取决于一个目前被认为相对无害且不重要的人。这将具有新科学发展的性质。股价会上涨，三年内大幅上涨，然后趋于平稳，再下跌。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: There is a constant interaction in the plant, between its parts, that you do not perceive. The leaves now present are biologically valid, interrelating in your terms. Yet in time terms each leaf is also aware of the past history of the plant, and biologically they spring up from that "past."
quotes.translation: 植物各部分之间存在着你们无法察觉的持续互动。以你们的说法，当前的叶片在生物学上是有效的，彼此相互关联。然而从时间角度来看，每片叶子也知晓植物的过往历史，并且从那个"过去"中生物学地萌发出来。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 731
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Each reader, therefore, has inner senses, and to some extent uses them constantly, though he is not aware of doing so at an egotistical level. Now, we use the inner senses quite freely and consciously. If you were to do so, then you would perceive the same kind of environment in which I have my existence. You would see an uncamouflaged situation, in which events and form were free and not stuck in a jellylike mold of time. You could see, for example, your present living room not only as a conglomeration of permanent-appearing furniture, but switch your focus and see the immense and constant dance of molecules and other particles that compose the various objects.
quotes.translation: 因此，每位读者都拥有内在感官，并在某种程度上持续使用它们，尽管在自我层面并未意识到这一点。而我们则相当自由且有意识地使用内在感官。如果你们也能如此，就会看到我所存在的同样环境。你们会看到一种未经伪装的情境，其中事件和形态是自由的，而非凝固在果冻般的时间模具中。例如，你们不仅能看到当下的客厅是看似永久的家具集合体，还能切换焦点，看到构成各种物体的分子和其他粒子那巨大而持续的舞蹈。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You could (underlined twice) walk into "yesterday" as well as tomorrow at that point of birth -- if you could walk -- and indeed your perception brings you events both in and out of time sequence. Responses to out-of-time events do not bring the infant recognition, approval, or action, however. It immediately begins to learn to accept certain neurological pulses which bring results, and not others, and so neurological patterns are early learned. This can be a frightening process, though it is accompanied by reassurances. The infant sees, out of context, both present and future without discrimination, and (intently) I am speaking of images physically perceived.
quotes.translation: 在出生那一刻，你（双下划线强调）可以走入"昨天"如同走入明天——如果你能行走的话——而事实上你的感知为你带来时间序列内外的事件。然而，对非时间事件的反应不会为婴儿带来认知、认可或行动。它立即开始学习接受某些能产生结果的神经脉冲，而非其他脉冲，因此神经模式很早就被习得。这可能是一个令人恐惧的过程，尽管伴随着安抚。婴儿在脱离上下文的情况下，不加区分地同时看到现在和未来，（专注地）我说的是物理感知到的影像。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 688
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: Creativity rides the tides of love. When love is denied its natural expression, creativity suffers. Your beliefs lead you to suppose that a natural bisexuality would result in the death of the family, the destruction of morals, rampant sexual crimes, and the loss of sexual identity. I would say, however, that my last sentence adequately describes your present situation (with dry humor). The acceptance of the species' natural bisexuality would ultimately help solve not only those problems but many others, including the large instances of violence, and acts of murder. In your terms, however, and in your circumstances, there is not apt to be any easy transition.
quotes.translation: 创造力乘着爱的潮汐。当爱被剥夺其自然表达时，创造力便受损害。你们的信念使你们认为，自然的双性倾向会导致家庭解体、道德沦丧、性犯罪猖獗以及性别认同的丧失。而我要说，我最后一句话恰如其分地描述了你们目前的处境（带着冷幽默）。接受物种自然的双性倾向，最终不仅有助于解决这些问题，还能解决许多其他问题，包括大量的暴力事件和谋杀行为。然而，就你们的说法和处境而言，不太可能轻易实现转变。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: And in each of these planes of existence there is a reflection of the basic laws of the spacious present itself, which I am in the process of giving you. Therefore there is no need, really, to think of a given group of entities before the birth of your planet. I have said that all the entities who would ever dwell upon your plane did exist, and actually have a hand in on the creation of your planet, that would ever dwell upon it.
quotes.translation: 在每一个存在层面上，都反映了广袤现在本身的基本法则——我正逐步向你们阐述这些法则。因此，实际上没有必要认为在你们星球诞生之前存在某个特定的实体群体。我曾说过，所有曾经、正在或将要居住在你们层面的实体，在你们星球创造之时确实存在，并且实际上参与了这一创造过程。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: (Smile, eyes open.) Short range predictions would be most difficult for me, since I cannot break down time as you think of it to that degree. I perceive instead at one glance seemingly whole segments of your time field. (Pause.) Seth as you knew him perceives your own system more clearly. (Pause.) From my viewpoint (pause) I do not perceive your physical universe then as you do. I do perceive your psychic values and Emotional intensities, and realize that you perceive but very small segments of these. As you break down the spacious present into moments, which you then experience, so also you break down psychic identity, gestalts, into small self structures.
quotes.translation: （微笑，睁眼。）短期预测对我来说最为困难，因为我无法像你们所想的那样将时间分解到那种程度。相反，我一眼就能感知你们时间场中看似完整的片段。（停顿。）你们所认识的赛斯能更清晰地感知你们的系统。（停顿。）从我的视角来看（停顿），我并非像你们那样感知你们的物质宇宙。我确实感知到你们的心灵价值和情感强度，并意识到你们只能感知到这些的极小片段。正如你们将广袤现在分解成一个个瞬间来体验，你们也将心灵身份、完形结构分解成微小的自我结构。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: It is also possible that these secondary personalities, emerging, give access to buried abilities and benign constructive abilities which had been buried beneath heavier fears that weighed them down. In any case this level represents the last division of what we may call the present personal subconscious, and still only accounts for a small dimension of the inner self, being composed of only the most easily accessible portions.
quotes.translation: 这些次人格的出现，也可能让人接触到被沉重恐惧所掩埋的潜藏能力与良性建设性能力。无论如何，这个层面代表了我们所谓当前个人潜意识的最末划分，且仅由最易触及的部分构成，仅占内在自性的微小维度。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Now. He enjoys having sessions to some degree for his students, for he responds to their desire and need, but he distrusts himself more when you are not present, you see. (Long pause.) This should vanish in time. It is a new experience for him. In such cases, you see, he must trust himself entirely to me, and since he distrusts himself and me to some varying degree, then there is some conflict. This is not of a severe nature.
quotes.translation: 现在。他某种程度上享受为学生们举办课，因为他回应他们的渴望与需求，但当你不在场时，他更加不信任自己，你看。（长停顿。）这应会随时间消失。对他而言这是新体验。在这种情况下，你看，他必须完全将自己托付给我，而由于他对自己和我的信任程度各有不同，便产生了些许冲突。这并不严重。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: Latent potentialities, always present within the system, are activated as they are needed. The physical self can, it is true, become somewhat bewildered, but the ego also learns and expands so that it is able to accept and use the new information, and benefit from it. It quickly learns methods for example to utilize such activities for the purposes of the psychological structures that it recognizes and feels responsible for.
quotes.translation: 潜在的可能性始终存在于系统之中，在需要时便会激活。确实，物质自我可能会变得有些困惑，但自我也会学习并扩展，从而能够接受并运用新信息，并从中获益。例如，它会迅速学会利用此类活动的方法，以服务于它所认知并感到负有责任的心理结构。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: First of all you must understand, again, that now you do not realize your true identity. You identify instead with your present ego, so when you think in terms of life after death you really mean a future life of the ego that you know. At the end of the reincarnational cycle you understand quite thoroughly that you, the basic identity, the inner core of your being, is more than the sum of your reincarnational personalities.
quotes.translation: 首先，你必须再次明白，此刻你并未意识到自己的真实身份。你反而认同于当前的自我，因此当你思考死后生命时，你实际上指的是你所知的自我在未来的一种生命。在转世循环的终点，你会彻底明白，你——基本身份、你存在的内在核心——远不止是你所有转世人格的总和。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: All of the ages of Earth, in your terms both past and present, exist, as do future ages. Now. You may make that a capital now. Some life forms are being developed in what you think of as present time. They will not appear physically until you reach your future time. Do you follow me?
quotes.translation: 地球的所有时代，用你的说法包括过去和现在，都存在着，未来的时代也是如此。此刻。你可以把“此刻”大写。有些生命形式正在你所认为的“现在时间”中发展。它们直到你到达未来时间时才会以物质形态出现。你明白我的意思吗？
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: It is a materialization of an inner framework, an inner psychic framework. Many other personality structures do not need a materialized perceptive frame-work, such as this, but an inner psychic organization is always present. It also represents the personality's present capabilities.
quotes.translation: 这是内在架构、内在心灵架构的物质化。许多其他人格结构并不需要像这样的物质化感知架构，但内在心灵组织始终存在。它也代表了人格当前的能力。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: "You" are not divorced from these other streams of consciousness in any basic way; only your focus of attention closes you off from them, and from the events in which they are involved. If you think of your stream of consciousness as transparent, however, then you can learn to look through and beneath it to others that lie in other beds of reality. You can also learn to rise above your present stream of consciousness and perceive others that run, for analogy's sake, parallel. The point is that you are only limited to the self you know if you think that you are, and if you do not realize that that self is far from your entire identity.
quotes.translation: “你”在任何基本层面上都与这些其他意识流没有分离；只是你的注意力焦点将你与它们隔绝开来，也与它们所涉及的事件隔绝开来。然而，如果你将你的意识流视为透明的，那么你可以学会看穿它、看到它之下，从而触及位于其他实相河床中的意识流。你也可以学会超越你当前的意识流，感知那些（打个比方）平行流动的其他意识流。关键在于，只有当你认为自己仅限于你所知的自我，并且没有意识到那个自我远非你的全部身份时，你才会受到限制。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Some correspondents write: "I realize that I am too egotistical." There are many schools for spiritual advancement that teach you to "get rid of the clutter of your impulses and desires," to shove aside the self that you are in search of a greater idealized version.
